tasks.description: The `existing_artifact_ids()` function in `src/artifact.rs` currently rejects any directory whose name is not all-ASCII digits. YouTube video IDs are 11-character alphanumeric strings (e.g., `jNQXAC9IVRw`) — this filter makes YouTube artifacts invisible to `scan_artifact_statuses`, `show_status`, and `transcribe-all`. Replace the all-digit check with a `status.json` presence check: any subdirectory that contains `status.json` is a valid artifact. This is the de-facto artifact contract since S01's normalization and is backward-compatible with all Twitch artifacts. Also update the affected unit test (`test_scan_queue_dedup_with_artifact`) which creates artifact dir `300/` without a `status.json`; under the new rule, `300/` won't appear in results. Fix: add `status.json` to the `300/` setup so the test's dedup-verification intent still holds.

tasks.title: Wire YouTube URL routing into download_vod and produce a complete artifact
tasks.description: Add hostname-based URL routing to `download_vod` in `src/main.rs`. At the very top of `download_vod`, before calling `twitch::extract_video_id`, parse the URL hostname using the already-imported `url` crate. If the host contains `youtube.com` or `youtu.be`, call a new async helper `download_youtube_vod(video_link, output_root)` and return its result. Otherwise, fall through to the existing Twitch path unchanged. Implement `download_youtube_vod(url: &str, output_root: &Path) -> Result<PathBuf, Box<dyn std::error::Error>>` as an async fn in the same file: (1) call `ytdlp::download_youtube(url, &audio_path)` where `audio_path` is a temporary path (use a temp file or the final artifact path — see note below); (2) extract `video_id` from `meta.id`; (3) build `artifact_dir = output_root.join(&video_id)`; (4) call `artifact::prepare_artifact_dir(&artifact_dir)`; (5) call `artifact::write_source_url(&artifact_dir, url)`; (6) move/verify `audio.m4a` is at `artifact_dir/audio.m4a` (yt-dlp was told to write to this path directly); (7) call `artifact::ArtifactMetadata::from_ytdlp(&video_id, url, &artifact_dir.join("audio.m4a"), &meta)`; (8) call `artifact::write_metadata(&artifact_dir, &metadata)`; (9) write `ProcessStatus` with `downloaded: true` via `artifact::write_status`. Note on output path: pass `artifact_dir.join("audio.m4a")` to `download_youtube` — yt-dlp will write directly there. decisions.decision: Product framing
decisions.choice: Reframe from a Twitch-only downloader into a queue-first, artifact-first media ingestion pipeline
decisions.rationale: The real workflow is media-to-transcript processing, not isolated downloading

decisions.decision: Core workflow model
decisions.choice: Center the CLI on durable per-item artifact/job state
decisions.rationale: The user wants unattended progress, resume behavior, and clear failure visibility

decisions.decision: Transcript default bar
decisions.choice: Bias toward trustworthy transcript output over raw speed
decisions.rationale: Downstream notes and memory work depend on transcript trustworthiness
decisions.revisable: Yes — if a later hybrid strategy proves equally trustworthy

decisions.decision: Cleanup behavior
decisions.choice: Cleanup is explicit operator action via candidate review, not automatic deletion
decisions.rationale: Source deletion is destructive and should remain under manual control

decisions.decision: Milestone split
decisions.choice: M001 proves reliable intake-to-transcript; M002 adds notes and Ember; M003 adds source expansion and polish
decisions.rationale: This sequences trust-critical backbone work before downstream and expansion work

decisions.decision: How to handle discrepancy between task summaries and actual slice delivery for S01
decisions.choice: Document the gap honestly in slice summary; record what was actually delivered vs claimed; mark incomplete work as blocking for downstream slices
decisions.rationale: Task summaries for T01/T02/T03 claim complex lifecycle types (JobLifecycleState, StageLifecycleState, etc.), three regression tests, and a status CLI command. Actual implementation uses simple boolean flags, zero tests, and no status command. Writing a dishonest slice summary would hide critical gaps (missing status CLI and test coverage) from downstream agents and from the human planner. Documenting the truth in the summary allows S02 to proceed using the durable queue/status files while flagging that status CLI is a critical blocker for human UAT and milestone sign-off."

decisions.decision: How to decouple download from transcription stages
decisions.choice: Extract into composable async helpers and add batch CLI commands that read persisted queue/artifact state
decisions.rationale: Enables operators to queue work once, then download and transcribe at different times. Persistent status allows resumption on failures.

decisions.decision: Error handling in batch processing commands
decisions.choice: Implement --continue-on-error flag to allow partial progress; failed items logged and persisted in status.json
decisions.rationale: Allows long-running batch operations to continue despite transient failures. Users can inspect failures later and retry selectively.

decisions.decision: R003 - Downloading must continue making progress even when transcription is pending
decisions.choice: Validated by S02 implementation
decisions.rationale: download-all and transcribe-all commands decouple these stages; download can proceed independently via download-all while transcription remains pending

decisions.decision: R012 - Interrupted or partial work must be resumable without redoing completed stages
decisions.choice: Validated by S02 implementation
decisions.rationale: Batch commands filter by artifact status; download-all skips already-downloaded items; transcribe-all skips already-transcribed items. Status.json persists across invocations.

decisions.decision: Single-video queue intake UX
decisions.choice: Separate `queue-video <url>` command; infers channel from Twitch API, merges into queues/<channel>.json
decisions.rationale: Keeps channel-queue structure consistent so download-all picks up queue-video entries automatically. Avoids polluting `queue` with a different intake mode.

decisions.decision: download-all channel argument optionality
decisions.choice: Channel argument becomes an optional filter; omitting it walks all queue files and merges them
decisions.rationale: Operators should not need to remember channel names to drain the queue. Optional channel acts as a filter for targeted runs.

decisions.decision: Display metadata schema ownership
decisions.choice: Title, uploaded_at, and channel live in metadata.json only; status command reads both metadata.json and status.json per artifact for display
decisions.rationale: Keeps schemas clean and single-purpose. The extra per-artifact read is acceptable; duplicating display fields into status.json would create a sync burden.

decisions.decision: Queued-but-not-downloaded item visibility in status
decisions.choice: Show queued items mixed in by default; no flag needed to see them
decisions.rationale: Operators want a single view of everything in flight. Hiding queued items behind a flag creates a mental model mismatch.

decisions.decision: Metadata fetch ordering in bare download path
decisions.choice: GQL metadata fetch occurs after stream resolution but before prepare_artifact_dir
decisions.rationale: Ensures no orphan artifact directories are created if metadata fetch fails. Allows download to abort cleanly with error message and recovery hint (--skip-metadata) before any filesystem side effects. Preserves artifact directory invariant: if a directory exists, it has at least metadata.json and status.json.

decisions.decision: Context threading pattern for metadata propagation
decisions.choice: Pass vod_context as Option<(&str, &str, &str)> (borrowed str references); fetch returns owned Strings, convert to borrows for from_download
decisions.rationale: Avoids lifetime parameter cascades through the call stack (download_vod -> from_download -> various callers). Owned Strings from GQL fetch are temporary and can be safely borrowed within download_vod scope. Simpler to read and maintain than lifetime-parameterized closures or context structs.

decisions.decision: Artifact normalization: bare download writes status.json
decisions.choice: Both bare download and queue-backed download paths write status.json with downloaded=true after media download completes
decisions.rationale: Normalizes artifact structure across intake paths. Operators can run status command on artifacts from either path (download <url> or download-all after queue-video). S02 status display depends on this invariant. Removes a class of edge cases where status command would fail or produce confusing output.

decisions.decision: Status column layout: STAGE replaces DOWNLOADED boolean
decisions.choice: STAGE shows human-readable tokens (queued, downloaded, ready, failed, suspect) derived from status.json and media file presence
decisions.rationale: Operators need to immediately understand item state without reading status.json. The boolean DOWNLOADED was machine-oriented; STAGE is human-readable. Derives state from multiple sources (downloaded flag, transcribed flag, transcription_outcome, ready_for_notes) to surface the current lifecycle phase. Supports future transitions (S05 filtering on STAGE, S06 retry handling on STAGE classification).

decisions.decision: Queued items merged into default status view
decisions.choice: scan_queue_files walks all queues/*.json and merges VodEntry items into status output by default; no --filter flag required
decisions.rationale: Operators want a single unified view of all work in flight (queued, downloading, transcribed). Hiding queued items behind a flag creates a mental model mismatch and forces operators to remember to check queue files separately. Deduplication by video_id ensures merged display is accurate when a queued item moves to artifact directory. Precondition for S03 (Intake Flexibility) and S05 (Queue-Aware Filtering).

decisions.decision: Deduplication rule in merged queue+artifact status view
decisions.choice: When a video_id appears in both queue files and artifact directories, the artifact-dir row is displayed and the queue entry is filtered out
decisions.rationale: Artifact directory indicates the item has progressed beyond queueing (has metadata, status, or media file). Artifact row has richer state (STAGE, OUTCOME, REASON). Showing the same video_id twice would confuse the operator. Artifact row always wins because it represents the most current state. Implementation: collect artifact IDs in HashSet, filter queued items by membership before display.

decisions.decision: Missing metadata.json fields render as em dash (—), not [missing] or empty string
decisions.choice: Any missing title, channel, or uploaded_at field defaults to em dash (—) in status display
decisions.rationale: Graceful degradation pattern: items without complete metadata.json still appear in status output (no panic, no hard error). Em dash is visually consistent, clearly indicates missing data, and takes minimal space. Supports pre-S01 bare-download artifacts (media file present, metadata.json missing) and edge cases where metadata fetch failed but download succeeded.

decisions.decision: Sorting deferred from S02; rows appear in filesystem walk order
decisions.choice: No sorting controls in S02. Rows appear in the order they are discovered by filesystem walk (queued items from scan_queue_files, then artifact items from scan_artifact_statuses). TODO comment added for future sort-by-date-desc enhancement.
decisions.rationale: Sorting adds complexity and is not a must-have for S02. Default ordering is sufficient for verifying slice correctness. Sort-by-date-desc or sort-by-stage will be easy to add later (sort the merged Vec before display). Keeping sort deferred reduces risk and allows S02 to focus on human-readable display of data. Explicit TODO in code makes it easy to locate the sort site when needed.

decisions.decision: No --verbose flag for S02 REASON column
decisions.choice: REASON column is truncated to 35 chars inline in default status output; no separate --verbose flag added
decisions.rationale: Task plan Q7 asked whether REASON column would cause width overflow. Testing shows 6-column table with aggressive truncation fits comfortably at 105-char line width, well under the 130-char standard terminal width. REASON truncation to 35 chars is sufficient to surface failure reasons (e.g., "Audio extraction timeout", "Transcript too short"). Full reason text is available in status.json if operators need the complete message. Simpler than adding a --verbose flag; reduces CLI surface area.

decisions.decision: Filtering strategy for --video-id feature in batch commands
decisions.choice: Implement filtering at handler level (download_all/transcribe_all functions) rather than at CLI argument validation level
decisions.rationale: Handler-level filtering preserves queue file and artifact directory integrity (no mutations), enables flexible composition of future filters without CLI parsing changes, and keeps the filter logic visible in the same place where pending-item logic lives. This pattern scales cleanly to S05's multi-filter needs (--filter queued|failed|transcribed).

decisions.decision: Rust crate for blocking exclusive file locking in write_status()
decisions.choice: fs4 v0.13 with `sync` feature
decisions.rationale: fs4 is more actively maintained than fs2 (last updated ~11 months ago vs ~8 years), has higher recent download velocity, uses rustix instead of libc as its system interface, and provides the same lock_exclusive() API via the FileExt trait. The sync feature surface is sufficient — async locking is not needed. fs2 is effectively stale at v0.4.3 with no recent activity.

decisions.decision: Choice of file locking mechanism for write_status() mutual exclusion
decisions.choice: Use fs4 crate (v0.13) with blocking exclusive locks via fs4::fs_std::FileExt
decisions.rationale: fs4 provides cross-platform, OS-level advisory file locking (POSIX fcntl on Unix, LockFileEx on Windows). Blocking exclusive locks are simpler than reader-writer locks for status.json writes since all callers serialize updates and last-writer-wins is acceptable behavior. RAII semantics (lock drops automatically at scope end) prevent accidental unlock misses. Avoids complexity of std::sync::Mutex on the shared status struct itself, which would require defensive locking everywhere ProcessStatus is accessed.

decisions.decision: File locking mechanism for status.json concurrent write protection
decisions.choice: fs4 crate v0.13 with exclusive lock_exclusive() blocking method
decisions.rationale: fs4 is actively maintained, provides portable cross-platform file locking (macOS, Linux, Windows), uses OS-level flock/LockFile, and integrates cleanly via RAII. Alternative file-lock crate required manual unlock() calls and add complexity. fs4's blocking lock_exclusive() matches the requirement: writer waits without error or warning, no try-lock fallback.

decisions.decision: yt-dlp invocation strategy for YouTube download
decisions.choice: Two-pass: metadata first with `--dump-json --skip-download`, then download with `-x --audio-format m4a -o <path> --no-part`
decisions.rationale: Two-pass gives clean JSON parsing from stdout (no progress output mixed in) and a known deterministic output filename. Single-pass mixing `--print-json` with download output is fragile because yt-dlp progress lines can interleave with the JSON blob. Two-pass is slightly slower but far more reliable.

decisions.decision: yt-dlp audio format flag for YouTube downloads
decisions.choice: `-x --audio-format m4a` (post-processing via ffmpeg)
decisions.rationale: More reliable across all videos than `-f bestaudio[ext=m4a]` (format selection). Many YouTube videos do not have a native m4a audio stream; format selection would silently fall back to webm/opus which `hear` does not accept. Post-processing via ffmpeg ensures the output is always m4a. ffmpeg is already a required dependency for Twitch downloads.

decisions.decision: Artifact discovery strategy in `existing_artifact_ids()`
decisions.choice: Replace all-ASCII-digit filter with `status.json` presence check: any subdirectory containing `status.json` is a valid artifact
decisions.rationale: YouTube video IDs are 11-character alphanumeric strings (e.g., `jNQXAC9IVRw`) — the all-digit filter rejects them entirely, making YouTube artifacts invisible to `status` and `transcribe-all`. The `status.json` presence check is the de-facto artifact contract (all artifact dirs have had `status.json` since S01's normalization). `queues/` subdirectory has no `status.json`, so it won't pollute results. The fix is purely additive and backward-compatible with all existing Twitch artifacts. The test `test_scan_queue_dedup_with_artifact` must be updated: artifact dir `300/` has no `status.json` and will no longer appear in results; add `status.json` to `300/` to preserve the test's dedup-verification intent.
